perturbation.original_text: "Discloser" means a party to this agreement which discloses or makes available directly or indirectly Confidential Information. "Purpose" means considering whether or not to enter into a b usiness collaboration between the parties and putting into place the arrangements for that collaboration. 2 "Recipient" means a party to this agreement that receives or obtains directly or indirectly Confidential Information. "Representative" means, in relat ion to a party, that party's employees, agents, accountants and lawyers.
perturbation.changed_text: "Discloser" means a party to this agreement which discloses or makes available directly or indirectly Confidential Information. "Purpose" means the evaluation of a potential business collaboration. 2 "Recipient" means a party to this agreement that receives or obtains directly or indirectly Confidential Information. "Representative" means solely the direct employees of a party.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Purpose' is narrowed to only the 'evaluation of a potential business collaboration,' excluding the 'putting into place the arrangements for that collaboration' part. This creates a contradiction with clauses that expect the 'Purpose' to include more than just evaluation, specifically related to putting the agreements into place for collaboration. Also, by changing the 'Representative' means solely the direct employees of a party, contradicts 4.1, making it impossible to comply, due to 4.1.4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient may disclose all or part of the Discloser's Confidential Information to a Representative who needs to know that particular Confidential Information for the Pur pose provided that, in relation to each Representative to whom the Discloser's Confidential Information is to be disclosed, the Recipient prior to disclosure: 4.1.1 informs the Representative of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; 4.1.2 informs the Discloser of the identity of the Representative to whom it intends to disclose Confidential Information; 4.1.3 procures that the Representative shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed, comply with this agreement as if it were the Recipient; and 4.1.4 if the Discloser so requests, in relation to a Representative other than an employee of the Recipient, procures that such Representative enters into a confidentiality agreement with the Discloser on terms equivalent to those contained in this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Representative' to include 'solely the direct employees of a party.' The contradicted text, specifically 4.1.4, requires the Recipient to procure a confidentiality agreement from a Representative 'other than an employee of the Recipient.' This directly contradicts the revised definition, as 'Representative' now exclusively refers to employees, making it impossible to comply with the requirement for Representatives who are not employees. Also the Purpose was also narrowed which may create a contradiction with the Contradicted Text where the Purpose is less strict.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall, at all times, be liable for the failure of any of its Representatives to comply with the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall not be liable for the failure of any of its Representatives to comply with the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original text. The original text enforces strict liability on the Recipient for their Representatives' actions, while the modified text removes this liability entirely. This creates immediate uncertainty in enforcement, especially concerning breaches of confidentiality by Representatives. This contradicts the intention behind clauses 3 and 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Discloser or any of its Representatives disclosing or making available Confidential Information to the Recipient or any of its Representatives, the Recipient, on behalf of itself and its Representatives, undertakes to comply with the terms of this agreement. The Recipient shall, at all times, be liable for the failure of any of its Representatives to comply with the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Recipient 'shall, at all times, be liable'. The changed text states the Recipient 'shall not be liable'. The contradicted text also states the Recipient 'shall, at all times, be liable'. The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by negating the Recipient's liability, which conflicts with the contradicted text's explicit statement of liability, causing inconsistency in enforcement. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because it creates conflicting obligations regarding the Recipient's responsibility.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall keep the Discloser's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Discloser or as expressly permitted by this agreement, shall: 3.1.1 not use the Confidential Information except for the Purpose; 3.1.2 not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party; 3.1.3 not make any copies, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion in whole or in part except as strictly necessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Discloser); 3.1.4 keep the Confidential Information in a safe and secure place and establish and main tain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorised access or use, including but not limited to implementing any reasonable security measures proposed by the Discloser from time to time; and 3.1.5 at the written requ est of the Discloser, or in any event on termination of this agreement, return or destroy all documents or other records containing Confidential Information to the Discloser and confirm to the Discloser that it has done so in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall use reasonable efforts to keep the Discloser's Confidential Information confidential and, except as commercially unreasonable, shall: 3.1.1 use the Confidential Information as they deem necessary; 3.1.2 disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party; 3.1.3 make any copies, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion in whole or in part as strictly necessary for their convenience (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Recipient); 3.1.4 keep the Confidential Information in a safe and secure place; and 3.1.5 upon the Discloser's request, return documents.
perturbation.explanation: This completely undermines the confidentiality obligations. By replacing 'shall keep' with 'use reasonable efforts,' 'not use except for the Purpose' with 'use as they deem necessary,' 'not disclose' with 'disclose,' and giving ownership of copies to the Recipient, the core purpose of the NDA is negated. This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement and renders clause 5.1 useless since they now claim ownership. 3.1.5 is also a contradiction, making the request optional
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the property of th e party disclosing it, which reserves all rights in its Confidential Information. No rights, including, but not limited to, any and all intellectual property rights, in respect of a party's Confidential Information are granted to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several points of contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text requires the Recipient to keep information confidential, use it only for the Purpose, and prevents disclosure. The changed text weakens these obligations, allowing the Recipient to use information as they deem necessary and disclose it to third parties, which directly contradicts the intent of keeping information confidential and owned by the discloser, as stated in the contradicted text (clause 5.1). Furthermore, the changed text gives ownership of copies to the Recipient, which goes against the contradicted section that all confidential information remains the property of the disclosing party and reserves all rights.

perturbation.original_text: Termination of this agreement shall not affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: Termination of this agreement shall affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled.
perturbation.explanation: The altered text directly reverses the original meaning, creating an explicit contradiction. The original clause preserves rights and remedies even after termination, while the altered clause eliminates them. This adds uncertainty regarding the enforceability of obligations after the agreement ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either party decides not to continue to be involved in the Purpose with the other party it shall notify the other party in writing and this agreement will terminate w ith immediate effect. The obligations of each party shall continue for a period of [ Insert number of years - this should be a realistic estimate of how long it is likely that the information that is to be disclosed will remain important to protect, e.g. 5 ] years from the termination of this agreement. Termination of this agreement shall not affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly reverses the original meaning of the clause regarding accrued rights and remedies upon termination. The original text states that termination 'shall not affect' these rights, while the changed text states it 'shall affect' them. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which explicitly states that termination does not affect accrued rights, leading to uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party irrevocably agrees to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Belgium over any claim or matter arising under or in connection with this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Belgium over any claim or matter arising under or in connection with this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive,' the agreement now allows claims to be pursued in jurisdictions other than Belgium. This contradicts the original intent of ensuring all disputes are resolved in Belgium, creating uncertainty about where legal action may be initiated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party irrevocably agrees to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Belgium over any claim or matter arising under or in connection with this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'exclusive jurisdiction' in Belgium. The changed text modifies this to 'non-exclusive jurisdiction,' directly contradicting the contradicted text which also specifies 'exclusive jurisdiction.' This creates a clear and direct conflict, as the change allows for legal action outside of Belgium, undermining the original agreement's intent. As per the definition for 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' this creates an in-text contradiction.
